inline_comments.message: I dont know the kubectl syntax - why replace instead of apply ?

The old logic was to delete the default storage class, then apply a new dummy storage class, but on occasion Azure would still re-create the default storage class in between those two actions, and that would cause the ONAP install to fail (because the default Azure class uses azure disk).

I'm hoping using replace will solve that. I tried originally using apply, but it was throwing an error like seen here https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/80472.

it won't solve it.
FYI, I've worked on a "real" deployment of ONAP with use of storage class (3 but it can be 2) that _should_ work on AKS.
If you're interested, I can show you what I'm doing

Yeah I'd be interested - is that in a repo you can point to? 

Can you clarify what you mean that it won't solve it? The other logic worked the majority of the time. I'm trying to improve it so it works every time.
